title: Anointed People
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}} {{Species |image=[[File:AnointedPeople-AE.png]] |name=Anointed People |designation=[[Sentience/Legends|Sentient]]{{CSWECite|I|6; 32|Abonshee" and "Anointed People}} |class=[[Reptile/Legends|Reptile]] |subspecies= |races= |height=1.5–2.5 [[Meter/Legends|meters]]{{WEGCite|story=Tests of the Godking|book=Star Wars Campaign Pack}} |length= |wingspan= |mass= |skincolor=[[Color/Legends|Green]] |haircolor=None''[[Alien Encounters]]'' |feathers= |eyecolor=Black |distinctions=Spiked tail |lifespan= |origin=[[Abonshee]] |habitat= |diet= |language=[[Anointed People's language]] }} {{Quote|The Masterhome expedition, the only one ever sent to that planet, reported that the Anointed People were bloodthirsty primitives living in a feudal state almost lacking in advanced technology. The Empire has long ignored Masterhome; we cannot.|Santhou Lazith'chika|}} The '''Anointed People''' were a [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Reptile/Legends|reptiles]] [[Homeworld/Legends|native]] to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Abonshee]]. Their bodies were sturdy and roughly [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]], standing on two [[Limb/Legends|feet]] with a tail used for balance. Members of the species used sharp [[Tooth/Legends|teeth]] and a spiked tail as natural weapons and hard, [[Color/Legends|green]] skin as [[Armor/Legends|armor]]. As of [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]], the Anointed People had a feudal society divided into castes, including the working-class [[Unwashed]] and the ruling [[Godling]]s, all serving a planetary [[King/Legends|monarch]] known as the [[Godking]]. The [[galactic community]] believed them to be a primitive and hostile species, based on obsolete [[Scout/Legends|scouting]] reports from the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] that portrayed the Anointed People as a non-technological, unsophisticated species that constantly waged civil wars. However, soon after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], representatives of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] rediscovered the Anointed People and found them to be intelligent and sophisticated, if still not technological. By that point, the Anointed People, mostly under the rule of Godking [[Trisstan]], had made important social advances, replacing their constant warring with a sport-like activity. The [[Operative|agents]] of the Alliance tried to convince the Anointed People to join their faction in the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], but Godking Trisstan was reluctant to agree: he had recently been the victim of [[Jayhawk]], a [[Con artist/Legends|con artist]] who claimed to be a member of the Alliance. Trisstan tested the might and the nature of the [[Alien/Legends|aliens]] before deciding. The Anointed People eventually joined the Alliance in their fight against the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and [[Battle of Abonshee|fought]] Imperial [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] when the Empire landed on Abonshee. ==Biology and appearance== The self-designated Anointed People were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] that evolved on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Abonshee]]. The Anointed People were [[Reptile/Legends|reptiles]], like many of the animal lifeforms on their planet, and like most reptiles, they lacked hair. Although their precursor species were [[Limb/Legends|four-legged]], non-shelled, scaled animals, they evolved into [[Humanoid/Legends|anthropoids]] that walked on two legs—with their long tail providing additional balance. They also evolved strong [[Bone/Legends|bones]] that protected them from bludgeoning damage. The Anointed People had natural weapons in the form of a thagomizer—a spiked tail—and that of big, pointed [[Tooth/Legends|teeth]] in their narrow, long heads. They had hard, [[Color/Legends|green]] skin that provided them with a natural [[Armor/Legends|armor]] against physical and, to a lesser extent, energetic damage. Generally stronger and taller than [[Human/Legends|Humans]], members of the species ranged in height from 1.5 to 2.5 [[Meter/Legends|meters]]. As a norm, the Anointed People were clumsier and less dexterous than Humans. ==Society and culture== ===Hierarchy=== {{Quote|Space guys, huh? ''[sighs]'' Just what I need. ''[Aide whispers in his ear]'' Huh? Oh, right. As lord high Godking of the most holy Anointed People, I most graciously welcome you travelers from a most distant land to the blessed shores of Masterhome, etc., etc. ''[sic]'' So, what's on your mind?|Godking Trisstan|}} During the last [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and until at least the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the Anointed People lived in a feudal society, primitive by [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] standards.{{CSWECite|I|32|Anointed People}} [[Politics/Legends|Political]] power was centralized in the hands of one individual, known as the [[Godking]]. This person's subjects belonged to two classes: the [[Unwashed]] and the [[Godling]]s. The Unwashed were stout serfs and workers, while the Godlings were [[Noble/Legends|aristocrats]], ruling over a number of Unwashed individuals—mostly through verbose and meaningless speeches—and in turn obeying the Godking's orders. Some Godlings also occupied roles as the Godking's [[Aide/Legends|aides]]. Both groups favored brightly colored clothing; the Godlings enjoyed expensive scale armor while the Unwashed were permitted to wear only common robes. As of [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]], parts of their grandiose pomp and nobiliary titles were only symbolic remnants of a previous age, lacking any practical value. The aristocracy kept the Unwashed content but uneducated, and they were unaware of life on other planets beyond their native Abonshee, a place called "Masterhome" by the locals. Consequently, the Unwashed concentrated their leisure time in domestic ventures and entertainment, such as drinking distilled beverages and [[Gambling/Legends|gambling]]. In contrast, the Godlings were aware of intelligent life from beyond the [[Star/Legends|stars]] and understood that it could be a great opportunity for them; at the same time the Godlings were frightened by their limited knowledge of life beyond the stars, including ignorance of the goals and abilities of those [[Alien/Legends|aliens]]. As such, the Godlings were commonly cautious when dealing with alien sentients, directing them to the Godking. The Godking dynasties were selected from among the Unwashed families. The Godking was supposed to retain power only as long as he could stop any Godling from overthrowing him. As such, the Godking commonly tried to manipulate ambitious aristocrats into infighting and internal rivalries. ===Technology=== With a medieval technological level, and no proof of more advanced technology on their planet, the Anointed People were technologically unsophisticated by the standards of [[Realspace/Legends|spacefaring]] cultures. They used [[Wagon/Legends|wagons]] pulled by draft animals and had the usual problems of pre-industrial societies, including poor medical care and a high rate of infant [[Death/Legends|mortality]]. [[File:Anointed_People_architecture.png|thumb|left|Construction was primitive among the Anointed People.]] With their limited mechanization, the Anointed People built homes from stone and wood, commonly a one- or two-floor house or [[Farm/Legends|farm]] in a forest clearing. The Godlings lived in small [[Castle/Legends|castles]], built as military structures and usually surrounded by five-meter high walls and a moat, with a personal guard of fifteen People. The castle was commonly built near some [[water well]] or a path crossing, and some two dozen houses surrounded it, forming a town. Individual farms could be beyond the sight of the castle. The greatest [[City/Legends|city]] of the northern hemisphere, which was the seat of the Godking government, was inhabited by twenty thousand Anointed People, spaced across six square [[Kilometer/Legends|kilometers]]. The Godking lived in a [[Godking's castle|palace]], three times as large as any Godling castle, baroquely decorated with tapestries and full of courtesans and pages. Its guard also tripled the defense complement of any Godling. For large engagements, the Anointed People used [[Catapult/Legends|catapults]] and ballistas. Typical melee weapons used by the Godlings included [[Sword/Legends|broadswords]] and shields, while the Unwashed had to make do even more primitive [[Club/Legends|clubs]]. For hunting, some [[Gender/Legends|males]] used only a bag and a [[Spear/Legends|spear]]; with just these tools, they still manage to capture forest-dwelling [[Griff (creature)|griffs]], whose meat was considered a delicacy. However, none of this gear was enough to deal with the gargantuan local predator known as the [[eater]]. Some wealthy males rode the native [[likka]], using reigns tied to the beast's [[Eye/Legends|eyelids]]. The Anointed People used [[Gold/Legends|gold]] and jewelry in their treasure, and [[Silver/Legends|silver]] to build the crown of the Godking. ===Customs=== Although not technologically sophisticated, the Anointed People had a complex culture. Having previously been a warlike people, they adapted and changed to minimize [[Blood/Legends|bloodshed]] as much as possible—in part, because they considered physical bloodshed to be aesthetically unpleasant, but also because they erroneously believed advanced civilizations, such as a galaxy-spanning one, would naturally tend to be peaceful. By the time of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], the Anointed People had renounced waging violent civil wars against each other. However, due to their tendency to respect and maintain ancient traditions, the Anointed People evolved this practice by fighting mock battles. Every spring, almost every Godling would recruit his or her vassals, equip them, and go to a neighboring castle. The marching [[Army/Legends|army]] would then formally challenge the other Godling to battle and wait for that person to arrange his or her own army. The resulting battle used only clubs—no edged weapons—meaning that the strong bones of the Anointed People protected them from permanent damage; the bludgeoning weapons rarely caused real injuries, and any damage inflicted was comparable to what might be expected from a sports match. Eventually, one of the belligerents would surrender or retire. Custom dictated that the losing Godling pay tribute to the victor, commonly sponsoring a three-day fiesta for both armies. The Unwashed particularly enjoyed these events, taking the opportunity to dress in comical clothes, see other lands, meet potential mates, and most of all, thrill at the final jamboree. The Anointed People had a [[Anointed People's language|language]] that outsiders could learn using recorded tapes. ==History== {{Quote|Looks like we've joined the Rebellion after all. Now, how do we get at those butchers down there?|Godking Trisstan|}} [[File:GodkingsCastle-SWCampaignPack.png|thumb|right|Prisons within the Godking's castle]] The Anointed People evolved on Abonshee, a temperate planet with moderate seasonal variation and abundant and diverse plant and animal life. Located in the [[Colonies/Legends|Colonies]]' [[Fakir sector/Legends|Fakir sector]],{{CSWECite|I|6|Abonshee}} the planet was well outside the [[Sector/Legends|sector's]] [[Hyperspace route/Legends|trade routes]]. From reptiles with four legs and scales, they developed into two-legged humanoids with a spiked tail and natural armor in the form of hard skin and strong bones. Traditionally a bloodthirsty, militaristic species, the Anointed People were frequently involved in armed territorial disputes during their early history. As a consequence, they constructed fortified strongholds, with villages clustering around such structures for protection. Power became centralized in the Godking; however, the position was coveted and so the Godking was almost constantly trying to quell rebellions. Circa [[89 BBY]], a [[Scout/Legends|scout]] [[Starship/Legends|starship]] of the Galactic Republic visited Abonshee, met the locals, and received a hostile reception. The explorers recorded their observations of the Anointed People, including their technology level, social structure, and hawkish attitude. Their [[Computer/Legends|computer]] tapes also included a translation program for the local language and a warning for visitors to the planet. Soon afterward, around [[74 BBY]], the last real war on Abonshee ended, and the practice was replaced with the spring tradition of simulating a war. Around [[39 BBY/Legends|39 BBY]], a crafty individual known as [[Trisstan]] became the Godking of Abonshee. Initiating a visionary taxation policy and good public relations, Trisstan brought unparalleled freedom and prosperity to the world. ===The Rebel Alliance=== In [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], the Galactic Empire became the governing body of the galaxy.{{Film|III}} The Empire sent no forces to Abonshee, believing the planet lacked any notable resources and that the reportedly truculent Anointed People would require a significant investment of time and forces to pacify. Around the time of the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], Abonshee was visited by a Human known as [[Jayhawk]]. This offworlder presented himself as a representative of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] who wanted to meet and negotiate with the Godking. In reality, Jayhawk was a [[Con artist/Legends|con artist]], with no affiliation to the Alliance. He claimed that the Alliance wanted the Anointed People to join the Rebellion against the Empire and offered to exchange an [[Elixer of eternal life|elixir of eternal life]] for their support. Godking Trisstan met with Jayhawk for several days, until Jayhawk killed two guards, stole hundreds of [[Kilogram/Legends|kilograms]] of [[Gold/Legends|gold]] and jewels from the royal treasury, and left the planet at night. Realizing he had been deceived by the Human, Trisstan silenced any reference to this event. {{RPG}} [[File:TheLongShot.jpg|thumb|left|The crew of the ''Long Shot'' made contact with the Anointed People.]] Contemporaneously, the Alliance—unaware of Jayhawk's deceitful activities—sought to enlist Abonshee in their rebellion. The Alliance representatives in the Fakir sector, [[Reekeene's Roughnecks]], sent a starship, the ''[[Long Shot]]'', containing [[Green squad]], to open [[Diplomat/Legends|diplomatic]] relations with the Anointed People and to buy food in exchange for technology and qualified staff—munitions, however, were excluded from their tradeable commodities. The Anointed People greeted the Rebels with civility to the surprise of their guests, who, provided only with the obsolete data of the Old Republic explorers, expected to find bellicose locals. The Rebels were directed to the Godking to draft a treaty. The Godking, still smarting from his encounter with Jayhawk, had reservations over trusting the newcomers. Accordingly, Trisstan decided to clandestinely test their rectitude: should their resulting behavior be innocent, he would apologize, reveal the history of his encounter with Jayhawk, and place his support behind the Rebellion. However, if their behavior revealed deceit, he would [[Prison/Legends|imprison]] them. He tested the foreigners through questioning and other means to determine their honesty, physical strength, greed, alertness, technology, and skills. For instance, on Trisstan's secret orders, the Godling [[Satrank]] pretended to be a conspirator wanting the Rebels to join him in overthrowing Trisstan. ===Imperial intervention=== While Green squad was negotiating on Abonshee, the [[Resurgence (Curich-class)|''Resurgence'']], an Imperial [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle]] that was ferrying [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] [[Dixton]], [[Moff/Legends|Governor General]] of the Fakir sector, and his entourage, suffered damage in a [[Skirmish near Abonshee|skirmish]] and was forced to land on the world to perform necessary repairs. The crew of the ''Long Shot'' detected the shuttle arriving and escaped to [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]], leaving the diplomats behind but promising to return as soon as they could get some military reinforcements. The shuttle landed, and some thirty [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] were deployed around it. Seeing this, Godking Trisstan sent Godling [[Grissom (Anointed People)|Grissom]] as his emissary, accompanied by an honor guard of twenty-five [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] of the Anointed People. The stormtroopers ordered the Godling to stop in the name of the [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] and, when Grissom did not comply, the [[Troop/Legends|troops]] fired on Grissom, the guards, and several nearby civilians. Hearing word of this unprovoked assault, Trisstan retreated to the palace and officially joined the Rebel Alliance in the name of Abonshee. [[File:Anointed_v_Stormtroopers.png|right|thumb|The Anointed People battle Imperial stormtroopers.]] Not a military strategist, Trisstan wanted to expel the invaders, but he could not come up with a better plan than a frontal assault with two hundred soldiers that would result in the death of seventy-five percent of the warriors. The Anointed People were prepared to fight even at such odds, but Trisstan appealed for any advice that could minimize casualties. Together, the Anointed People and the Rebel diplomats [[Battle of Abonshee|battled]] the Imperials and, eventually, were joined by the returning ''Long Shot'' with two [[X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing starfighters]] and one [[Light cruiser]], achieving victory against the invaders. The Anointed People received all the visiting Rebels with a large party, and the diplomats were proclaimed "[[Heroes of the Realm]]" and knighted "[[Assistant Demi-god]]s" before leaving the planet. {{Endgame}} ==Anointed People in the galaxy== {{Quote|Say, stranger. That's a neat…thing you have there. What's it do?''"
"''It's a starship. It travels between the stars.''"
"''Sounds interesting. But why would anyone want to? Something the matter with the star you came from?|A Godling and a member of the Rebel Alliance|}} Before their contact with the Alliance to Restore the Republic, the Anointed People had little presence outside of Abonshee. Afterward, the Alliance was happy to accept any individual wanting to join their army. Also from this time on, the Empire took interest in the Anointed People and went to the planetary surface of Abonshee to fight them.[[Star Wars Miniatures Battles (first edition)|''Star Wars Miniatures Battles'', First Edition]] Beyond that, the Anointed People showed little need to visit other planets or the space between them. The Godlings were uninterested in faraway places, and the Unwashed rarely believed in, or cared about, such things even when faced with them directly. ==Behind the scenes== The Anointed People first appeared in "[[Tests of the Godking]]," the first adventure in "[[The Long Shot Campaign]]," which was included in the [[Roleplaying game|role-playing]] book ''[[Star Wars Campaign Pack]]'', written by [[Paul Murphy]] and published by [[West End Games]] in [[1988]] for ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''. "The Long Shot Campaign" includes drafts of several adventures that the gamemaster can expand with more details; one of these, "Tests of the Godking," includes information on the Anointed People, presented both as a draft and as an already-expanded adventure, ready to be played. "The Long Shot Campaign" was republished in [[1994]], along with another campaign, in ''[[Classic Campaigns]]''. "Tests of the Godking" mentions that Jayhawk visited Abonshee several years before the events in the adventure, and then contradicts itself, saying that Jayhawk had visited Abonshee only [[Standard month/Legends|months]] before the events in the adventure. This article assumes that the event had been recent. The book [[Star Wars Miniatures Battles (first edition)|''Star Wars Miniatures Battles'']] ([[1991]]), also published by West End Games, includes an image of miniatures of the "Anointed People" fighting invading Imperial stormtroopers. The caption identifies the miniatures as "[[Wikipedia:Lizardfolk|lizard men]]," sculpted by [[Grenadier Models]] for a different role-playing game, Martial Metals, and [[Wikipedia:Ral Partha Enterprises, Inc.|Ral Partha]]. The cabin in the background is identified as a product of Gallia Reproductions, and the stormtroopers, not identified, are the only figures specifically made for the ''Star Wars'' roleplaying game; they were also sculpted by Grenadier Models, the authorized distributor for West End Games' ''Star Wars'' roleplaying game.Grenadier Models, Inc. is historically identified as such [http://www.cs.cmu.edu/~tpope/sol/west-end-games/star-wars.html here,] [http://www.parroom.net/Atelier/PSA_Sculpture.html here] [http://www.nobleknight.com/ViewProducts.asp_Q_ProductLineID_E_77_A_ManufacturerID_E_17_A_CategoryID_E_5_A_GenreID_E_0 and here]. The caption is not specific enough to define whether this is a graphic depiction of the events in "Tests of the Godking" or a representation of further happenings: "Tests of the Godking" describes that the battle is inevitable and that it would probably take place with the Anointed People attacking the Imperial headquarters around a shuttle, while ''Miniature Battles'' show Imperial stormtroopers attacking a house or a village of the Anointed People that does not coincide with the description of the [[Capital/Legends|capital]] city in "Tests of the Godking." However, "Tests of the Godking" does not describe the [[Battle of Abonshee]] in detail, and as such this article does not include information about the battle under "History." In [[1998]], ''[[Alien Encounters]]'' added new information to the background of the species and repeated some previously known data. No official source has revealed the reason for their name, why they consider themselves to be anointed, or the singular form for "Anointed People." ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Anointed People}} *{{WEGCite|story=Tests of the Godking|book=Star Wars Campaign Pack|reprint=1}} {{1st}} ==Sources== *[[Star Wars Miniatures Battles (first edition)|''Star Wars Miniatures Battles'', First Edition]] *[[Star Wars Miniatures Battles (Second Edition)|''Star Wars Miniatures Battles'', Second Edition]] *''[[Alien Encounters]]'' *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Colonies sentient species]] [[Category:Reptilian sentient species]] {{RelatedCategories |[[Category:Anointed People]] }}
